headline: Morning Brief: Goldman rakes it in, but issues a warning
summary: Despite a Hormuz blockade, an outlook warning from Goldman Sachs, and oil still surfing the $100 per barrel, the stock market resumed its progress up and to the right.

headline: CarMax Stock Sinks After Earnings. New CEO Has Work to Do.
summary: Shares of  CarMax  fell Tuesday after the used-car retailer reported another year-over-year drop in quarterly earnings and revenue.  CarMax  posted adjusted earnings in its fiscal fourth quarter of 34 cents a share, down from 58 cents a year ago but above analysts’ consensus call for 23 cents, according to FactSet.  CarMax stock declined 5.7%.

content.title: CarMax Stock Sinks After Earnings. New CEO Has Work to Do.

CarMax  posted adjusted earnings in its fiscal fourth quarter of 34 cents a share, down from 58 cents a year ago but above analysts’ consensus call for 23 cents, according to FactSet.  CarMax stock declined 5.7%.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Wells Fargo, JPMorgan, BlackRock, American, Credo, CarMax, J&J, and More
summary: Stock futures were mostly higher Tuesday as investors gathered hope for a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran and big banks ushered in the earnings season.  First-quarter net interest income came to $12.1 billion, missing analysts’ forecasts for $12.3 billion and marking a 2% decline from the previous quarter.  The world’s largest asset manager reported $2.2 billion in net income, marking a 46% jump from the same period last year.

content.title: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Wells Fargo, JPMorgan, BlackRock, American, Credo, CarMax, J&J, and More
content.summary: Stock futures were mostly higher Tuesday as investors gathered hope for a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran and big banks ushered in the earnings season.  First-quarter net interest income came to $12.1 billion, missing analysts’ forecasts for $12.3 billion and marking a 2% decline from the previous quarter.  The world’s largest asset manager reported $2.2 billion in net income, marking a 46% jump from the same period last year.

headline: CarMax (NYSE:KMX) Exceeds Q1 CY2026 Expectations But Stock Drops
summary: Used automotive vehicle retailer Carmax (NYSE:KMX) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, but sales were flat year on year at $5.95 billion. Its GAAP loss of $0.85 per share was significantly below analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: CarMax Drops as Investors Underwhelmed by Revival Strategy
summary: (Bloomberg) -- CarMax Inc.’s shares slumped as investors weren’t convinced about its turnaround strategy of cutting vehicle prices while increasing its cost-savings goal in a competitive market.Shares sunk as much as 17% in New York, the most since November. The stock was up 27% this year through Monday’s close, though the stock remains down more than 60% over the past five years. Investors were also disappointed by a pause in buybacks.The company has to lower its used-car prices to attract cust

content.summary: (Bloomberg) -- CarMax Inc.’s shares slumped as investors weren’t convinced about its turnaround strategy of cutting vehicle prices while increasing its cost-savings goal in a competitive market.Shares sunk as much as 17% in New York, the most since November. The stock was up 27% this year through Monday’s close, though the stock remains down more than 60% over the past five years. Investors were also disappointed by a pause in buybacks.The company has to lower its used-car prices to attract cust

headline: CarMax Stock Plunges After Earnings. New CEO Has Work to Do.
summary: Shares of  CarMax  fell sharply Tuesday after the used-car retailer reported another year-over-year drop in quarterly earnings and revenue and paused its stock buyback program.  CarMax  posted adjusted earnings in its fiscal fourth quarter of 34 cents a share, down from 58 cents a year ago but above analysts’ consensus call for 23 cents, according to FactSet.  CarMax stock plunged 13% after falling more modestly in premarket trading.

content.title: CarMax Stock Plunges After Earnings. New CEO Has Work to Do.
content.summary: Shares of  CarMax  fell sharply Tuesday after the used-car retailer reported another year-over-year drop in quarterly earnings and revenue and paused its stock buyback program.  CarMax  posted adjusted earnings in its fiscal fourth quarter of 34 cents a share, down from 58 cents a year ago but above analysts’ consensus call for 23 cents, according to FactSet.  CarMax stock plunged 13% after falling more modestly in premarket trading.

headline: Tesla stock upgraded, CarMax Q4 earnings disappoint Wall Street
summary: Yahoo Finance anchor Josh Lipton tracks Tuesday's top-moving stocks and biggest market stories. Tesla (TSLA) stock was upgraded to Neutral from Sell by UBS analysts. CarMax (KMX) stock is sinking after the company's fourth quarter earnings results disappointed Wall Street.

Yahoo Finance anchor Josh Lipton tracks Tuesday's top-moving stocks and biggest market stories.

Tesla (TSLA) stock was upgraded to Neutral from Sell by UBS analysts.

CarMax (KMX) stock is sinking after the company's fourth quarter earnings results disappointed Wall Street.

headline: Why CarMax Stock Is Sinking Despite Earnings Beat
summary: CarMax reported earnings for the fourth quarter on Tuesday on higher used car sales. But CarMax stock plunged below a key level intraday. The Carvana rival reported decreasing profits per unit sold. CarMax also reported pausing share buybacks in Q4 and recording a $141 million goodwill impairment charge.

content.title: Why CarMax Stock Is Sinking Despite Earnings Beat
content.summary: CarMax reported earnings for the fourth quarter on Tuesday on higher used car sales. But CarMax stock plunged below a key level intraday. The Carvana rival reported decreasing profits per unit sold. CarMax also reported pausing share buybacks in Q4 and recording a $141 million goodwill impairment charge.
